title: Conditional Rules and Syntax to Show Content
heading_path: Conditional Rules and Syntax to Show Content
text:
Look up the supported rules and proper syntax to create conditional content rules in
your Word documents for document generation.

In some use cases, you only want information to display if a certain condition is met. You can
use conditional logic rules to show text or other data fields.

title: Conditional Rules and Syntax to Show Content
heading_path: Conditional Rules and Syntax to Show Content
Format for conditional clauses to show information
text:
You can add logic rules to show information based on the value entered in a data
field.

`{{ if fieldName = 'value' }}`

`Text that appears if the sender inputs “value” for fieldName`

`{{ endif }}`

You can make a data field conditional on the value of another data field.

`{{ if fieldName = 'value' }} {{ ConditionalField }} {{ endif }}`

title: Conditional Rules and Syntax to Show Content
heading_path: Conditional Rules and Syntax to Show Content
Example: conditional clause based on state of residence
text:
For example, you have a data field {{ StateOfResidence }} in your document for the
client's state of residency. There is a clause in your document that should only
display if your client’s address is in California. You can ensure that clause only
shows up if that value is “California”:

`{{ if StateOfResidence = 'California' }}`

`Clause for Californians.`

`{{ endif }}`

If the sender enters "California" for the StateOfResidence data field, then the
generated document includes the specified clause.

title: Conditional Rules and Syntax to Show Content
heading_path: Conditional Rules and Syntax to Show Content
text:
General format
:   - {{ if Field\_Name comparison 'value' }}
    - Conditional fields can be created to require more than one rule is
      met, by combining the conditions with the word `and`
      .
      - Example: {{ if Field\_Name1 comparison ‘value1’ and
        Field\_Name2 comparison ‘value2’ }} ... endif
    - Conditional fields can be created to require at least one rule is
      met, by combining the conditions with the word
      `or`.
      - {{ if Field\_Name2 comparison ‘value’ or Field\_Name2
        comparison ‘value2’ }} ... endif

Text conditional fields
:   - Example: {{ if state = "CA" }} or {{ if state = 'CA' }}
    - Supported comparisons (case-sensitive):
      - Equals: =
      - Not Equal To: !=
    - Value can be in single or double quotes

Non-case-sensitive text conditional fields
:   For comparisons that are not case-sensitive, leverage the
    `$uppercase` operator on the field label.
:   - Example: {{ if $uppercase(state) = 'CA' }}
      - This would evaluate to True if the user inputs any of the
        following values: CA, Ca, ca, cA

Number conditional fields
:   - Example: {{ if $number(amount) > 100 }}
    - The comparison value cannot contain a comma separator
    - Supported comparisons:
      - Equals: =
      - Not Equal To: !=
      - Comparisons: >, >=, <, <=

Other rules
:   - The order of the conditional statement must always be: {{ Field comparison 'Value'
      }}
    - You cannot do: {{ 'Value' comparison Field }}
      - Example of invalid order: {{ if 'CA' = state }}
    - The {{ if }} clause indicates the start of the if clause and the {{
      endif }} indicates the end of the if clause. Everything within those
      two flags will be conditional on the value that is provided by the
      user.
      - Examples of acceptable formats:
        - {{ if fieldName == 'value' }} {{ ConditionalField }} {{ endif }}
        - {{ if fieldName == 'value' }}

          {{ ConditionalField }}

          {{ endif }}

title: Troubleshooting Syntax Errors With Document Generation Templates
heading_path: Troubleshooting Syntax Errors With Document Generation Templates
text:
Get help with common problems encountered when creating Docusign eSignature templates
for document generation. These errors pertain to syntax errors in uploaded .docx files.

When uploading a .docx file with document generation syntax to a Docusign eSignature template,
if there are any syntax errors you will get an error message. The message states the
number of syntax errors and the file will not upload. To resolve the errors, update the
.docx file to fix the syntax and re-upload. For details on the specific errors, download
the provided JSON file.

In the troubleshooting topics that follow, common syntax errors are described to help you
identify and correct them.

![Example of syntax errors shown during template creation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/docgentemplateerror.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMzcsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.cG-LQJKG97mOUOehKTYGh8TsycWpGATSbduF08FAg3g&_LANG=enus "Example of syntax errors shown during template creation.")

title: Limitations and Considerations for Document Generation
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Document Generation
text: Important information to consider before starting to develop document generation documents and templates.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Document Generation
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Document Generation
text:
Before you begin developing document generation templates, review this list of limitations and other consideration. Docusign will continue to develop this feature, and potentially address some of these limitations in future releases.

Note: Many of these limitations apply specifically to the [Agreement Template Builder for Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=jct1697745471160.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Agreement Template Builder and how it can help you edit your .docx files to create document generation templates.") (ATB), an editor option for creating a documentation generation template.

Supported file types
:   You can only use a .docx file to create a template for document generation.

    For envelope templates only: You cannot use ATB or WTA if you use any additional file types in your template. In ATB, you do have the option of creating a new document to add to your template. This creates a blank document that you use to build your content directly in ATB.

Supported file size
:   ATB supports files up to 1 MB in size. If you upload a .docx file larger than 1 MB, you will be directed to the standard template editor.

Documents per envelope template
:   ATB supports up to five (5) documents per envelope template. No set limit for the Template Assistant add-on.

Uploading documents with syntax
:   For Word documents containing document generation syntax, you must use the Word Template Assistant to upload them to Docusign, as described in [this help topic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=qwk1702687799547.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to prepare a DOCX file and upload it to Docusign using the Template Assistant to create a document generation template."). If you attempt to upload a document with syntax directly to a Docusign template, the syntax will not be recognized.

Local file source
:   Documents for ATB must originate from a local storage source. You cannot upload files to your template from cloud sources, such as Google Drive.

PowerForms
:   Document generation does not support PowerForms.

Field types
:   ATB supports the following sender field types: Text, Number, Dropdown, and Date. For the Date field, ATB currently supports MM/dd/yyyy. The Template Assistant supports three date formats.

Access to form data
:   For the initial release, the document generation field data entered by the sender is not accessible for separate download.

    Note: Document generation form field data is accessible through the API. See [this API reference](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/reference/envelopes/documentgeneration/getenvelopedocgenformfields/) for details.

Direct template use only
:   Document generation templates created with ATB or WTA cannot be applied directly to an envelope. You must use the template directly to create an envelope.

Correcting
:   When correcting an envelope, once a recipient finishes signing, or if the first recipient in the routing order is a Receives a Copy (CC) recipient, you can no longer edit the document generation form data. Likewise, you cannot add another document generation template to the envelope in this scenario.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Document Generation
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Document Generation
text:
Here is some additional information to consider before you develop document generation documents and templates.

Account setting
:   The Sending Settings option Enable Agreement Template Builder must be enabled for your account by your account administrator. This account setting is documented in [this administrator guide on template usage settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=edh1583277351016.html&_LANG=enus).

    Two additional Sending Settings options for AI features are required to enable [agreement type detection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=con143e1ca0-b763-4e21-8183-d3f964492b32.html&_LANG=enus) and AI-assisted field suggestions.

    ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/ai_sending_settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwMzUsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.kn1wK0i-roTVSd-GiZEV9AWmNNheEJ9N5I_kW9SnQ88&_LANG=enus)

User permissions
:   There are no unique document generation user permissions. The permissions required to create or use document generation templates are the same as for standard templates. See the Templates section of the [Permission Profile Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mfo1583277360424.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

Bulk send
:   Document generation supports bulk send, allowing you to use a document generation template and import a bulk list to send many envelopes at once. With bulk send, the sender does not enter custom data in a data-entry form prior to sending. Instead, the values for the document generation data fields come from the uploaded bulk send list. See the [Bulk Send With Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=nan1681853631175.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use bulk send with a document generation template.") guide.

Sender field data is limited to a single document
:   Sender field data does not replicate across template documents. For example, a user is sending a template that has two documents, each with a sender field called “Address”. The sender will have to complete the value for "Address" twice in the web form filling page.
:   In addition, sender fields are specific to a single file. If you add a sender field to one template document, it can be repeated in that document, but it is not available to add to any other documents in the template.

title: Agreement Type Detection
text:
ATB uses AI to detect automatically the agreement type of the document you upload when building a
template. The agreement type classifies your document and surfaces relevant data fields.
You can apply these fields to your template to insert data placeholders for senders to
complete when generating agreements.

The agreement type is also stored with completed documents in Docusign Agreement Manager. Agreement Manager helps you unlock data usage and visibility. Your account administrator manages agreement types and fields in Agreement Manager and can remove and add new types.
Administrators with the necessary permissions can access these controls from Agreement Manager > Manage Agreement Types. For details, see [this help topic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ypm1727282359686.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Agreement Type Detection
text:
When you create a template with ATB and upload your document, the content is analyzed. There are several pre-defined agreement types which should cover the majority of agreements. You can select a type or use AI detection to choose which type to apply to your document. There's also a "Miscellaneous" type if you don't find a suitable match.

You have the option to change the agreement type selected by the AI detection. While the AI is designed to provide high-quality detection and classification, there may be instances where manual adjustments are beneficial. This flexibility ensures the most accurate classification of the document, tailored to your specific needs or preferences.

Agreement fields are the fields associated with the agreement type. You can use these dynamic
fields to customize generated agreements. Agreement fields are assigned to the
Sender role and act as placeholders for data that will be provided by the preparer.
You can add new custom fields to an agreement type. You can also select any custom
field created on the account by you or other account users. Adding or selecting
other custom fields creates an association with the current agreement type. This
association means the fields will be included by default the next time you use that
type.

Note: For a list of the standard agreement types provided by Docusign and
their associated fields, see [this help topic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=smm1727282189861.html&_LANG=enus).

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/agreement_fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNjMsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.QQvLM3hOryeo9oTZF4chLI6elOHjY4MU_R6X8vGgiMg&_LANG=enus)

title: Choosing Between Document Editors
text:
Read about the editor options that you have for creating agreements.

When creating document generation templates in Docusign using a Word document, you have two
editor options: Agreement Template Builder (ATB) and Word Template Assistant (WTA).
These editors are a central part of Agreement Preparation and provide the ability to
edit fully the contents of your Word document. You also use them to add recipient,
sender, and third-party fields.

Understanding the differences between these editors will help you select the one that best fits your needs.

title: Choosing Between Document Editors
text:
Here's a high-level summary of the options that you have to create a template from a Word document:

Word Template Assistant (WTA)
:   WTA is designed for advanced users who need robust customization and
    flexibility. It offers a comprehensive set of features for building
    complex templates and workflows. WTA is a plug-in for Microsoft Word
    and provides the rich, native controls of that application. The WTA
    offers advanced syntax control over your content.

    Use this Microsoft Word add-in to help you build finished files for
    document generation in Docusign eSignature. It
    assists with adding sender fields, building rules to drive
    conditional content, and offers a preview option to check results.
    Upload finished files to Docusign as a document template or
    eSignature template.

Agreement Template Builder (ATB)
:   ATB provides a streamlined, user-friendly interface for quickly
    creating and editing templates. It is ideal for users who prefer
    simplicity and speed over advanced customization. ATB is an
    integrated feature within the Docusign application and provides a
    simple, streamlined method to prepare templates.

    With ATB, you edit template documents directly in Docusign to create
    and edit document generation templates. Just upload a .docx file to
    your template or, for envelope templates, create a new document
    directly in the template. Add sender and signer fields, define
    conditional logic rules to show or hide content, and preview your
    results with customized content.

Table 1. Key differences between ATB and WTA

| Feature/Capability | WTA | ATB |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Customization level | High | Moderate |
| User interface | Advanced, feature-rich | Simple, intuitive |
| Best for | Complex templates, power users | Standard templates, new user |

title: Choosing Between Document Editors
heading_path: How to choose the right editor
text:
Consider the following when choosing between WTA and ATB to create your template:

- **Template Complexity:**

  - Use WTA if you need to create complex templates with advanced logic. WTA also supports inline
    conditional rules, which allow you to place conditions on text
    within a paragraph so you can show or hide content.
  - Choose ATB for straightforward templates that don’t require extensive customization.
- **User Experience:**

  - If you are new to document templates or prefer a simple interface, ATB is likely the better choice.
  - If you are comfortable with advanced features and need more control, WTA is recommended.
- **Team Needs:**

  - ATB: Ideal for teams with diverse experience levels, promoting standardized template creation.
  - WTA: Best suited for specialized teams or workflows requiring greater flexibility.

title: About the Template Assistant
text:
Read about the Word Template Assistant. The Microsoft Word add-in helps you create, test, and upload dynamic agreement templates to your Docusign account using fields, rules, code, and preview tools.

The Docusign Word Template Assistant is a Microsoft Word add-in that helps you create, test, and upload dynamic agreement templates to your Docusign account.

title: About the Template Assistant
text:
The Fields tab allows you browse and search for specific data fields that will be populated when an agreement is generated.

- **Search icon (Magnifying glass):** Quickly find a specific field by name.
- **Agreement type (Edit icon):** Change the agreement type.
- **Fields/Tables toggle:** Insert fields relevant to the agreement type or a dynamic table.

Note: Dynamic tables in the Word Template Assistant support up to 20 columns. If your table requires more than 20 columns, consider splitting it into multiple tables.

title: About the Template Assistant
text:
The Rules tab makes your documents dynamic. Instead of having multiple versions of an agreement, you can have one document that changes its content automatically.

- **Visibility:**

  - **Show content:** The selected text in your template will only display if the rule is met.
  - **Hide content:** The selected text will disappear if the rule is met.
- **Field name:** Select the "trigger" field (for example, "State" or "Total Amount").
- **Operator:** Define the logic of the comparison. Common operators include:

  - **Matches (=):** The value must be exactly the same.
  - **Doesn't match (≠):** The value must be different.
  - **Greater than or equal to/Less than or equal to:** Used for numerical values.
- **Value:** The specific "answer" to trigger the rule (for example, if "State" matches "California").

title: About the Template Assistant
text:
The Code tab allows you view and edit the JSON data structure used for document generation.

Insert JSON: Paste or edit the JSON representation of your fields.

title: About the Template Assistant
text:
The Account tab displays the user and organization information connected to the Word add-in. This is vital for ensuring that the templates you build are being saved to the correct Docusign environment.

- **Name and email:** The identity of the user currently logged into the add-in.
- **Account number and ID:** The unique numerical and alphanumeric identifiers for your Docusign account. These are often required for troubleshooting with Docusign Support. When signing in to WTA, the system will always log in to your user's default account, which is set under "My preferences".
- **Company:** The name of the organization or "Sandbox" environment you are currently accessing.
- **Environment:** Displays Demo (for testing) or Production (for live documents).
- Disable Classic Mode: Blocks access to classic mode.
- Switch Account: If the logged-in user has multiple accounts, they can switch between them without logging out.
- Logout: Logs out of the current account session.

title: About the Template Assistant
text:
In classic mode, you use the same core tabs as the new experience: Fields, Tables, Rules, Code, and Account.

You can browse and insert fields, configure tables, build rules, manage JSON, and verify account details without changing your workflow.

In classic mode, fields are reusable only for the individual user and are not shared at the account level. In the new experience, fields are reusable across all users in the account.

You can browse and insert data fields, configure dynamic tables, build conditional logic, review or paste JSON, and confirm account details without changing your existing workflow.

Important: When you switch to the new experience, existing classic-mode fields are not carried over. You must create new fields in the new experience. In classic mode, fields are reusable only for the individual user and are not shared at the account level. In the new experience, fields are reusable across all users in the account.

title: About the Template Assistant
text:
This is the final step in the agreement preparation process, where you can test your work before uploading it to Docusign.

**Preview:** The standard testing tool that generates a mock version of your Word document, showing how fields and rules display after data is merged. Use it to catch formatting errors or broken logic early.

**Preview with AI:** An advanced testing feature that uses Docusign's AI to analyze the document for risks, inconsistencies, and missing data. It helps ensure that your agreement is legally robust and logically sound by simulating how the AI will interpret the final document.

title: About the Template Assistant
text:
Once you have finished adding your fields, setting your rules, and testing with the Preview tool, the Upload document button syncs your Word document to your Docusign account.

- **Primary function:** It uploads the current DOCX file as a new or updated template in your Docusign account.
- **Automation:** It carries over the technical syntax and field mapping that you created in the assistant, so you don't have to manually re-create those fields in the Docusign web app.

title: Generate Agreements
text:
This section covers how to generate customized agreements from a prepared template. These generated agreements can be sent for signature in an envelope or downloaded for other use cases.

Docusign IAM customers can generate an agreement from either a document template or an envelope
template. eSignature customers can use envelope templates only (see [Note](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=con38e467ca-8098-421b-96c0-4483e1313775.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=con38e467ca-8098-421b-96c0-4483e1313775__note_q5z_f5h_xwb) for
additional details). The processes for these two options are similar, but there are a
few distinctions to be aware of.

Note: The Document Generation
add-on for eSignature is required to send more than five trial envelopes from a
template that includes advanced document generation features. The add-on includes
additional functionality such as conditional content, dynamic tables, and API
sending. For more information about the Document Generation for eSignature add-on,
contact the [Docusign Sales team](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: Generate Agreements
text:
- You can only generate one document template at a time.
- For templates that include third-party data fields, such as Salesforce fields:

  - You must use Docusign Workflow Builder or some other embedded integration solution to generate the agreement. Templates with third-party fields cannot be generated directly from the Docusign web app.
  - If the selected data source record lacks required data or contains other data errors, you will
    need to update the information in the third-party system and
    then reselect the record to ensure that the most current values
    are used.

title: Sender and Signer Fields
text:
This topic explains the roles of sender and signer fields in templates used in Agreement Preparation.

Sender and signer fields are two key types of data placeholders used when building a template in Agreement Preparation.

- **Sender Fields:**

  Sender fields are essential for ensuring that agreements are populated with the correct data provided by the preparer. They are used to customize the generated agreement with accurate data, which can include information pulled from external systems like Salesforce.

  - These fields are placeholders for **data provided by the preparer (Sender Role)** when an agreement is generated.
  - Agreement Fields are the sender fields that are associated with the Agreement Type. Each agreement type has a set of standard fields, and also includes any user-defined custom fields. Agreement fields are account-based and available to all account users with the appropriate permissions.

    Note: Agreement types and fields are managed in Agreement Manager by users with the appropriate permissions. For details, see [this help topic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ypm1727282359686.html&_LANG=enus). Agreement Manager is available for Docusign IAM plans only.
- **Signer Fields (Recipient Fields):**

  Signer fields are crucial for enabling recipients to complete or take action on the agreement.

  - These fields are assigned to the **Recipient Role (Signer)**, who is the participant in the agreement.
  - They are the **standard signing fields** for the signer to complete or take action on.

The following screenshot provides an example of **sender** and **signer** fields within a template.

![Screenshot of a template document showing both sender and signer fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/sender_signer_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwMzAsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.gjkzA7nffbaci5Fe1h1gBrTbKGg7KQDuDUi_rF1Lr0o&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a template document showing both sender and signer fields.")

title: Use Data from Third-Party Systems
text: In this section, learn about how Docusign IAM customers can use data from third-party systems, such as Salesforce or Microsoft Dynamics, to generate dynamic content in generated documents.

title: Use Data from Third-Party Systems
heading_path: Install and connect a third-party account
text:
Before you can build templates that use data from your third-party account, your account administrator must install and authorize the relevant extension app. Supported extension apps are available in the Docusign App Center.

Note: The availability of specific integrations depends on your Docusign IAM plan. For information about which third-party systems are supported for your account, contact your Docusign Sales representative.

For basic information on how to install apps, see the help topic [Install Apps in the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus).

For detailed information on how to install and connect your Docusign account with your Salesforce organization, see the help topic [Install the Salesforce Application From the Docusign App Center﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=jxo1706295810553.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Use Data from Third-Party Systems
heading_path: Create templates with third-party sender fields
text:
Once the necessary extension app is installed, you can create templates with data fields from your third-party system of record.

This involves first selecting one **source object** from your connected system (for example, a
Salesforce object). The source object will serve as the primary source of data for the
agreement. You can select one source object only per template.

Once the source object is selected, you can insert fields from that object directly into your template. These fields act as dynamic placeholders that are automatically populated with data from a specific record in your third-party system when an agreement is generated.

You can also include fields from **related objects** to pull in a wider range of necessary
data. These are objects that have a direct relationship to your chosen source object.

When agreements are generated from the template, they are customized with accurate and up-to-date information from your system of record.

title: Use Data from Third-Party Systems
text:
While you can add third-party fields to a template using the Agreement Template Builder (ATB), generating the final document with live data requires an **orchestration layer** or an **embedded integration**.

Templates with third-party fields (for example, Salesforce) cannot be generated directly from the
Docusign web app. Because there is no manual "look up" for this data, you must use one of
the following:

- **Workflow Builder Integration:** Use Docusign Workflow Builder to build an automated process. You define the connection
  and use **workflow variables** to identify the source record. The workflow uses the
  connection to fetch data automatically during runtime.
- **Embedded Scenarios:** Use Docusign APIs to build a custom integration that programmatically passes record IDs and authentication tokens.

Note: If you attempt to generate an agreement directly from a template containing third-party fields, a warning banner will explain that the connection ID is missing. The message guides you to complete fields manually or use a supported solution, such as Docusign Workflow Builder.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/3p_banner.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNjYsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.dGtbXpH2OfA0ZhZbYC-SXkUzrawNGtHfENvhAD8d0Gk&_LANG=enus)

title: Use Data from Third-Party Systems
text:
- You can select one source object per document. (You can access additional source object fields by using related objects.)
- If you change the source object after inserting third-party fields, those fields will be removed from your document.
- Actual field values from your third-party account are not available in the ATB. You can preview
  your document with sample data that you enter manually. To pull in actual values, you
  must generate an agreement.
- Documents with third-party fields require a supported data connection (like Workflow Builder or API). Attempting to use these templates directly in the Docusign web app will trigger an error banner.

title: About Agreement Preparation
text:
Read about Agreement Preparation, a cornerstone of Docusign IAM that streamlines the creation of agreements across your organization.

Customer relationships are built on agreements. How they are prepared matters. Docusign Agreement Preparation enables customers to create and manage templates that result in professional agreements with accurate data—data that's connected across the entire agreement management process.

Agreement Preparation offers intuitive template creation, centralized template management, and integrations to external data sources to improve accuracy and efficiency.

Key capabilities:

- Create and manage templates using the Agreement Template Builder (ATB) or the Word Template Assistant (WTA)
- Connect data from preparation through the entire agreement management process with third-party systems connectivity and integrations.
- Generate professional-looking agreements with a single click.

Agreement Preparation streamlines how your organization creates agreements before they are sent for signature. Instead of manually copying content from old contracts, re‑entering data from multiple systems, or maintaining dozens of similar templates, Agreement Preparation gives you a centralized, connected way to build and manage templates and generate accurate, professional agreements at scale.

title: About Agreement Preparation
text:
Agreement Preparation focuses on the **"create" stage** of the agreement lifecycle: everything that happens between deciding an agreement is needed and sending it out for signature.

With Agreement Preparation, you can:

- **Create agreement templates** using intuitive, guided tools, including:

  - An online Agreement Template Builder (ATB) in the Docusign platform.
  - A Word-based plugin (Word Template Assistant) for teams who prefer working in Microsoft Word.
- **Manage templates centrally**, so you always know:

  - Which templates exist and where they are used.
  - That only current, approved language is available to users.
  - That updates are made in a single location and automatically reflected everywhere the template is used.
- **Generate agreements dynamically** that:

  - Pull in trusted data from systems of record like Salesforce or Microsoft Dynamics.
  - Apply approved, standardized language and formatting.
  - Use conditional logic and dynamic tables to reflect the details of each customer, product, or transaction.

title: About Agreement Preparation
text:
Many organizations face challenges in agreement creation due to scattered templates, reliance on error-prone copy-pasting, inconsistent data sources, and difficulties in ensuring the use of approved language. These issues result in wasted time, inconsistent terms, and increased compliance risks.

Agreement Preparation is built to address these issues by offering:

- **Simplified template creation and administration** - Create templates for simple or complex scenarios, then manage them in one place instead of maintaining many near‑duplicate versions.
- **Faster agreement cycles** - Generate accurate, customer-specific agreements quickly, reducing manual editing and back‑and‑forth between teams.
- **Improved accuracy and compliance** - Use connected data and approved language so that agreements are consistent with corporate, legal, and regulatory requirements.

title: About Agreement Preparation
text:
Using agreement preparation to build document generation templates, sets your organization up to produce professional-looking documents to send for signature. Document generation supports use cases that require customizing or personalizing documents before sending. The sender enters custom data using a data-entry form. The custom data is inserted seamlessly, generating finished documents that are highly customized. Signers receive the finished documents ready to review and sign.

There are two types of templates that you can build to power agreement preparation:

- Document templates - A reusable document with placeholders for data fields to customize the agreement, and recipient roles and fields. For more general information on document templates, see our [Document Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map332c3ebc-8fca-4fac-9b9f-c7206adc3985&topicId=conc8f06624-6ef6-40a3-bbc8-c5c7fe18746b.html&_LANG=enus) guide (Available for Docusign IAM plans only.)

- Envelope templates - A complete template for a Docusign eSignature envelope. They can contain multiple documents and document templates. For more general information on envelope templates, see our [Envelope Templates guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=dqj1578456412286.html&_LANG=enus)

Creating document generation templates is a core feature for Docusign IAM customers. Docusign eSignature customers can explore this functionality too and create eSignature templates with basic document generation features.

Note: All Docusign eSignature and Docusign IAM customers have access to create document generation templates. The Document Generation add-on for eSignature is required to send more than five trial envelopes from a document generation template that includes advanced features. The add-on includes additional functionality, such as [conditional content](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=ziz1707785438108.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to show or hide content dynamically based on values entered in sender fields."), [dynamic tables](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=zhi1724987563409.html&_LANG=enus "Read about dynamic tables. Dynamic tables are designed for agreements that include tables with a variable number of rows, depending on the data provided."), and [API sending](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/documents/document-generation/). For more information about the Document Generation for eSignature add-on, contact the  [Docusign Sales team](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: About Agreement Preparation
text:
Document generation is a valuable solution for high-volume use cases that require customizing or personalizing documents before sending. The potential applications are vast. Here are a few use cases to illustrate the value of document generation.

Vehicle leasing
:   A leasing agent enters the customer's personal information and lease terms in a form to generate a fully customized lease.

HR offer letter
:   A large enterprise hires hundreds of people each quarter. An HR specialist generates offer letters customized with each prospective employee's information. Candidates receive personalized offer letters, helping them feel like valued individuals. The entry form for the custom data makes it fast and easy to enter each candidate's offer details accurately. If the HR specialist needs to send many offer letters at once, they can use bulk send. Each letter is customized with the offer details coming from the bulk send list.

Detailed contracts
:   A major corporation with complex contracts needs to ensure they are completed accurately. The data-entry form simplifies the sending process, guiding the sender through a list of all the data required to customize the contract.

title: Autoplacing Suggested Fields
text:
AI autoplacement streamlines document template preparation by automatically identifying and positioning fields, reducing manual effort and accelerating template creation.

Autoplacing fields with AI streamlines the process of preparing document templates by
automatically identifying and positioning fields within your document. Instead of
manually dragging and dropping every data point, you can use AI-assisted field
suggestions to populate your template with the correct fields quickly.

title: Autoplacing Suggested Fields
text:
When you upload a DOCX file in the Agreement Template Builder (ATB) and select the AI icon, the AI scans the content for common agreement data points. It looks for specific text markers and contextual clues to suggest the best location for fields.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/dt-11.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNjEsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.4sU_jArN3HZT0I9ckn351i2ogC1JGhafGkkxarggtr4&_LANG=enus)

What are text markers? Text markers are optional placeholders that you can add to your
document to guide the AI toward precise field placement. While the AI can
detect fields automatically, using markers provides greater control over where specific fields are generated.

The AI recognizes common symbols such as:

- **Brackets:** `[[Effective Date]]`, `[[Customer Name]]`, `[[Sender Field]]`, or `[Sender Field]`
- **Braces:** `{{Signature}}`, `{{Date Signed}}`, `{Sender Field}`, or `{{Sender}}`
- **Less-than or greater-than sign:** `<Sender Field>`
- **Underlines**

By using these markers in your original document, you help the AI precisely map information to the correct fields in your Docusign account.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/dt-12.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNjEsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.4sU_jArN3HZT0I9ckn351i2ogC1JGhafGkkxarggtr4&_LANG=enus)

title: Autoplacing Suggested Fields
text:
The AI evaluates field suggestions against three levels of your account data:

- **Agreement type fields:** Matches content to standard or custom fields already associated
  with the selected agreement type.
- **Account-level fields:** Matches content to fields that exist in your account but are not yet
  linked to the current agreement type.
- **New field suggestions:** If no existing field matches, the AI can suggest creating a new custom field (supporting Text, Date, or Number formats).

title: Autoplacing Suggested Fields
text:
- **Supported field types:** The AI primarily suggests Text, Number, and Date fields.
- **Multiple signers:** The AI places Signature and Date fields as Unassigned to prevent
  incorrect attribution. You can manually assign these fields to
  the correct recipient during the review process.

title: Autoplacing Suggested Fields
text:
This feature is managed at the account level. Administrators can enable or disable AI-Assisted Field Suggestions for ATB on the Sending Settings page. When enabled, the feature is available to all users with permission to create document templates.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/dt-10.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNjEsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.4sU_jArN3HZT0I9ckn351i2ogC1JGhafGkkxarggtr4&_LANG=enus)

title: Error: Invalid Data Field Type
text:
Troubleshoot a document generation syntax error that occurs when a data field is
formatted incorrectly and results in an unrecognized tag type error.

If a data field is incorrectly formatted it may not be recognized as a valid field type.

Example: A space before the SuppressParagraph tag`{t: “
SuppressParagraph", e:"FieldName = 'value'"}`

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | {"tagIdentifier": "{t:\"SuppressParagraph \", e: \"FieldName = 'value'\"}", |
|  | "errorCode": "InvalidTagType", |
|  | "message": "Invalid Json tag type. -- Invalid json tag SuppressParagraph ."} |
```

Template creator

For the verbose syntax required for
SuppressParagraph, the exact name of the field type must be in quotes. Review
the tag identified in the error message and correct any formatting issues, such
as extra spaces.

title: Create a Sender Field in the Template Assistant
heading_path: Create a Sender Field in the Template Assistant
text: Create and configure sender fields in the Docusign Template Assistant to personalize data in templates.

title: Create a Sender Field in the Template Assistant
heading_path: Create a Sender Field in the Template Assistant
text:
- Download and install the Docusign Template Assistant for Word from the [Microsoft Office Store](https://marketplace.microsoft.com/en-us/product/office/wa200005367?tab=overview).
- Open Word and launch the Template Assistant add-in.
- In the Template Assistant, log in to a Docusign eSignature account. Access any Production or Demo environment account.

  Note: Once you log in, you can see your Docusign account information by selecting the Account icon in the Template Assistant.

Sender fields are dynamic placeholders for specific data, such as start and end dates. Add and configure sender fields in the Docusign Template Assistant to personalize templates.

The data automatically populates every linked field across the template, so you define fields once and reuse them as needed.

Once fields are added, they become available in the Docusign Template Assistant for use in any document.

1. In the Docusign Template Assistant, go to the Fields tab.
2. Select the + button and Create field.

   ![Create field dialog in the Template Assistant showing options to enter the field name, optional description, and select the field type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/create-field-WTA.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwODYsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.djjCH7oKTcy1Z9DKkLr__vTf5yd0Bxa-qWZ0Qy2R6Rc&_LANG=enus "Create field dialog in the Template Assistant showing options to enter the field name, optional description, and select the field type.")

   Field Name
   :   Enter a valid data field name with no spaces. Examples: "FieldName" or "Field\_Name"

   Field Description
   :   (Optional) Add a subtitle for the field name. The description is not included in the final document.

   Field Type
   :   Select the field type from the dropdown, such as text, date, dropdown, or number.
3. (Optional) Select the Required option to require the sender to enter a value for the field.
4. Select the Field Type for the field.
   - Text. A free text field.
   - Date. A date in one of these formats:

     MM/DD/YYYY

     DD/MM/YYYY

     YYYY/MM/DD
   - Number. A numeric value from a minimum of -999999999.99 to a maximum of 999999999.99 that can include:
     - An optional period as a decimal separator. Up to two digits of decimal precision are supported.
     - Optional commas as thousands separators.
     - A leading minus sign to indicate a negative value.
   - Dropdown. A predefined list of up to 250 values in Text, Date, or Number format, excluding semicolons.
5. Select Add.

The field is added to the Fields list.

Note: The list shows all fields in the Template Assistant, available for insertion into any document. For more information about inserting sender fields, see the [Insert Sender Fields and Dynamic Tables Into a Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=kgg1680241042480.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Send a Document Generation Envelope
text:
Learn how a sender uses a template for document generation.

You start a document generation envelope by using a document generation template. You can
either start an envelope and use the configured template; or you can use the
template directly to create the envelope for document generation. As the sender, you
then pre-fill the required information to generate the final document for
signature.

Note: For document generation, you must have the appropriate user
permissions to use templates. Also, you must have access to the prepared
template. Document templates requires the **Document Template Use** permission. For an envelope template, you must either be the template owner, or the owner must share the
template with you.

1. Start your envelope in one of the following ways:
   - **Start an envelope**. From the Agreements page, select Start > Envelopes > Use a Template. In the Select a template window, select All Templates to see all envelope templates you can access and all published document templates on the account. Choose any template marked with the DOCUMENT GENERATION badge.
   - **Use a document template**. Locate the template you want to use. Select the more actions menu and select Use Template. .
   - **Use an envelope template**. Locate the template that you want to use and select
     USE. Set up the envelope details and select Next.

     Note: Document
     generation templates are marked with a DOCUMENT
     GENERATION badge for easy identification.
2. Enter field data for your document. Complete all required fields and any optional ones as needed.

   - If you used a **document template**, the Add field data form appears alongside the document, generating a dynamic preview of your agreement as you enter data.
   - If you used an **envelope template**, you first complete the Add field data form, then select Next to review your generated agreement.

   The Add field data form displays for you to complete.![Screenshot of the Add field data form for the sender to enter data for the merge fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/prefillform.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMTQsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.uactzu29QK-RDbgGhKgrPx3qZyLa8G-qPNFZ1A3-5hQ&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Add field data form for the sender to enter data for the merge fields.")
3. If there are multiple document generation files in the envelope the add field
   data form presents the fields for each file separately.

   If
   documents have data fields with the same field name, the sender sees the field
   listed once for each document where it appears. They can enter the same or
   different values for each.

   If your document includes one or more dynamic tables, upload the CSV file with
   the table data. For more information, see the topic [Upload Field Data for a Dynamic Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=shq1713209582306.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to send a document generation envelope that includes a dynamic table. As the sender, you provide field data for the table by uploading a CSV file.").
4. Review the generated document, including the inserted data and any additional
   fields. If you need to edit any of the merged data, select
   BACK to return to the Add field
   data form and make your changes.
5. When you are finished, select SEND.

The envelope with your generated document is sent.

title: Why Can't I Use a Document Generation Template To Send an Envelope?
heading_path: Why Can't I Use a Document Generation Template To Send an Envelope?
text:
Review the reasons for why you are unable to send an envelope using a document
generation template.

When I prepare an envelope from a document generation template, the
SEND button is grayed out. I can't send the envelope.

Your eSignature account does not have the Document Generation Add-On. The template you are
using to send an envelope contains advanced functionality that requires the

Your account administrator can
contact the Docusign Sales team to inquire about purchasing
