title: Document Generation Syntax
text:
Read about the syntax and formatting rules and options for preparing Microsoft Word
.docx files to use with document generation.

Document generation starts with a properly-coded Microsoft Word .docx file. This file contains
the static contents for your eSignature document, plus the data fields to specify the
dynamic data for the sender to enter. You can create and edit documents in Microsoft
Word or any word processing application that supports saving documents in .docx format.
Then manually enter data fields and configure conditional rules.

Alternatively, you can use the [Docusign Template Assistant for Word](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=kyy1679958874136.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how the Docusign Template Assistant for Word simplifies the creation of DOCX files and templates."). This Word add-in assists
with adding data fields, building rules to drive conditional content, and offers a
preview option to check results before uploading the file to an eSignature template.

Important: You must use the Template Assistant to upload
documents prepared with syntax. If you attempt to upload a document with syntax directly
to a Docusign template, the syntax will not be recognized.

title: Document Generation Syntax
Document Generation Syntax
text:
The text format of data fields comes from the formatting in the Word document. For example,
the data field in the Tally example letter **{{Start\_Date}}** is in bold font. So
the value the sender enters for this field will be in bold in the final generated
document. You can do the same with other formatting controls, such as font style,
size, and color.

title: Document Generation Syntax
text:
You can construct conditional clauses to customize your generated document based on values
entered for merge fields. See [Conditional Rules and Syntax to Show Content](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=ahh1679704095661.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the supported rules and proper syntax to create conditional content rules in your Word documents for document generation.").

title: Document Generation Syntax
text:
A document generation document may contain multiple instances of the same data field,
that is, fields that use the same field name syntax. Fields with the same field name
are populated with the same value. Senders see just one field to complete on the
data-entry form. The value they enter is inserted into all instances of that data
field in the final, generated document.

Data fields across separate documents are not related. If there are multiple document
generation files in an envelope, the data-entry form presents the fields for each
file separately. If documents have data fields with the same field name, the sender
sees the field listed once for each document in which it appears. They can enter the
same or different values for each.

Note: When using bulk send with document generation, data fields
across separate documents remain linked. In this case, the same value must be used
for all data fields that use the same field name syntax. See [Limitations and Considerations for Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=cah1680213420771.html&_LANG=enus "Important information to consider before starting to develop document generation documents and templates.").

title: Document Generation Syntax
heading_path: Adding signature and other types of fields
text:
Initially, document generation for eSignature will support only text data fields. The
recommended approach for adding other types of eSignature fields is to use AutoPlace
functionality. AutoPlace adds fields near each occurrence of a given string of
characters. If you use conditional content rules, your final generated document can
vary significantly in length and layout. Placing fixed fields on your template
document could result in fields not appearing where you intended. AutoPlace places
fields precisely where the target text string indicates, no matter where it ends up
in your generated document.

You can place Signature or other fields near visible characters in your document, for
example, if your document has a signature area and text "Sign here". Or you can add
character strings in a font that matches the document background, making them
invisible. For more information, see the guide [Add
Fields Automatically with AutoPlace](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=ytr1578456405118.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Delegated Permissions
text:
Read about how account administrators assign limited administrator capabilities through
the Delegated Administration functionality.

As a Docusign account administrator, the Permission Profiles page lets you
manage the user permission profiles for your account. The Delegated Administration
functionality allows administrators to create Permission Profiles that have limited
administrator capabilities in addition to the standard user permissions.

Note: For a more in-depth discussion of use cases,
review the [Best Practices for eSignature Delegated Administration](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/km-guide-best-practices-for-esignature-delegated-administration/) white paper.

Note: If your account does not have Delegated
Administration enabled, the Permission Profiles dialog box only shows User Permissions. Refer
to [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to multiple users simultaneously.") for more information on User
Permissions.

Delegated Administration is available only for certain plans. If you do not
see the functionality described in this guide, contact Docusign Support to
determine if it can be enabled for your account.

title: Preview a Generated Document in the Template Assistant
heading_path: Preview a Generated Document in the Template Assistant
text:
Learn how to use the preview feature in the Template Assistant to ensure that your documents meet expectations by identifying and resolving issues.

The preview feature in the Docusign Template Assistant helps identify and resolve document issues, ensuring that final documents meet your expectations.

1. Prepare your Word document by adding sender fields, dynamic tables, and content rules.
2. In the Docusign Template Assistant, select the
   Preview icon.

   ![Preview icon in the Template Assistant toolbar](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/preview-icon-WTA.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMDQsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.scuY-uzVm5mPPmZQ2XVELqnX0T4_uzcDg38M9FbI8Ec&_LANG=enus "Preview icon in the Template Assistant toolbar")
3. Enter test values for sender and dynamic table fields, and select Preview.

   You can switch between a standard view and a highlighted data view. The document opens in a new window, showing the final version with test values. Rules are evaluated, and values are saved for future previews.
4. Edit test values to assess different rule outcomes. Repeat the task steps to modify values.

The preview shows the final version with sender fields and content rules applied. Verify the content and adjust as needed before finalizing the template.

title: Agreement Template Builder for Document Generation
heading_path: Agreement Template Builder for Document Generation
text:
Read about the Agreement Template Builder and how it can help you edit your .docx files
to create document generation templates.

The Agreement Template Builder (ATB) is a feature within the Docusign platform that allows you to edit template documents directly in the application. With these tools, you have comprehensive control over your document's content, extending beyond merely adding fields for data input.

The template builder is an alternative to using the [Docusign Template Assistant for Word](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=kyy1679958874136.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how the Docusign Template Assistant for Word simplifies the creation of DOCX files and templates.") and offers an improved user experience for creating
document generation templates.

Note: All accounts have access to use the template builder and create
document generation templates. Docusign IAM accounts can create document templates and envelope templates and have full access to document generation features. Docusign eSignature accounts can create envelope templates only, and the Document Generation add-on for eSignature is required
to send more than five trial envelopes from a document generation template that includes
advanced features. Refer to the [Limitations and Considerations for Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=cah1680213420771.html&_LANG=enus "Important information to consider before starting to develop document generation documents and templates.").

With ATB, you can prepare document generation templates quickly and
easily. The template builder offers the following key features:

- Build document templates or envelope templates.
- Add fields manually,or use AI-assisted field suggestion to quickly populate templates with the correct fields.
- Add sender fields for the preparer to insert dynamic data and generate customized, professional-looking
  agreements.
- Add traditional signer fields directly inline with text
  for recipients to complete, such as adding a signature or marking a
  checkbox.
- Edit your documents directly in Docusign; no need to use an external
  application.
- Add conditional logic rules to show or hide content based on the dynamic data entered by
  senders.
- Insert dynamic tables. Tables are populated automatically with as
  many rows as needed when the agreement is generated.

![Screenshot of the agreement template builder showing sample document with sender and signer fields inserted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/atb_overview.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwMzcsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.s5j52g4HY69LAkttZxMM2SFqT_09M644D7GsZ4Eprek&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the agreement template builder showing sample document with sender and signer fields inserted.")

Note: In order to use the new agreement template builder, the Sending
Settings for Enable Agreement Template Builder must be enabled
for your account by your account administrator. This account setting is documented in
[this administrator guide on template usage
settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=edh1583277351016.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Insert a Dynamic Table
text: Learn how to add a dynamic table with the agreement template builder. Dynamic tables can be populated with as many rows as necessary when the agreement is generated.

title: Insert a Dynamic Table
text:
- Your account administrator must enable the Enable Agreement Template Builder setting in the account Sending Settings > Templates.
- Create a template and upload your .docx file with your basic content or create a new document directly in the template.

With the agreement template builder, you can create dynamic tables to insert into your Word documents. Dynamic tables adjust to fit the data provided, adding as many rows as needed when an envelope is sent.

When a sender uses a template containing a dynamic table, they upload a CSV file with the table data. The dynamic table adjusts to fit the data inserted, adding as many table rows as needed. Dynamic tables become part of the generated document and are not editable by recipients.

For more information about dynamic tables, see the [Dynamic Tables for Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=zhi1724987563409.html&_LANG=enus "Read about dynamic tables. Dynamic tables are designed for agreements that include tables with a variable number of rows, depending on the data provided.") topic.

1. In the agreement template builder, position your cursor where you want to insert the dynamic table.
2. Select + Insert Items > Table > Dynamic Table and select the number of columns you want to use.

   ![Screenshot of the Insert menu cascade to insert a dynamic table with three columns.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/insert_dynamic_table.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNTAsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.d2qwK6f7IEh5vmVZqMTE08xFhFsaQkuRc_Wv6T5AlMU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Insert menu cascade to insert a dynamic table with three columns.")
3. In the Insert dynamic table window, define your table name and columns and select Insert Table.

   - Table name is used to identify the table to insert data. It is not part of the generated document and is not visible to recipients.
   - Field names represent the columns for your table. The table can have a maximum of 10 columns for either agreement fields or third-party data fields.

   ![Screenshot of the Insert dynamic table window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/definedynamictable.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNTAsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.d2qwK6f7IEh5vmVZqMTE08xFhFsaQkuRc_Wv6T5AlMU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Insert dynamic table window.")

   Restriction: Do not use the pipe delimiter ("|") in field names if you intend to do a bulk send with the document generation template. See the topic [Bulk Send With Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=nan1681853631175.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use bulk send with a document generation template.").
4. Select your inserted table and select table elements to apply formatting.

   - **Text formatting**. Use ATB text formatting tools to apply fonts and other settings, such as bold, font size, and text alignment.
   - **Table settings**. Use table controls to configure columns (remove or reorder), add color fill to cells, or remove borders.![Screenshot of an inserted dynamic table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/atbdynamictableexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNTAsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.d2qwK6f7IEh5vmVZqMTE08xFhFsaQkuRc_Wv6T5AlMU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of an inserted dynamic table.")

title: Insert a Dynamic Table
text:
jdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNTAsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.d2qwK6f7IEh5vmVZqMTE08xFhFsaQkuRc_Wv6T5AlMU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of an inserted dynamic table.")

Your dynamic table is inserted. The table definition is added to list of tables available to account users with the selected agreement type. When a sender uses the template, they provide a CSV with the table data. See the topic [Upload Field Data for a Dynamic Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=shq1713209582306.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to send a document generation envelope that includes a dynamic table. As the sender, you provide field data for the table by uploading a CSV file.") for details.

title: Insert Sender Fields and Dynamic Tables Into a Document
heading_path: Insert Sender Fields and Dynamic Tables Into a Document
text:
Learn how to insert sender fields and dynamic tables into a Word document using the DocusignTemplate Assistant.

After adding sender fields or dynamic tables to the Docusign Template Assistant, insert them into a Word document.

Note: When using the Docusign Template Assistant for Word to add eSignature fields (such as Signature, Initial, and Date Signed), dynamic content can cause fields to display in the wrong position. To keep fields aligned with your content, use **Anchor Tags** to attach fields to specific text strings. For more information, see the [What are Automatic Anchor Tags?](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/What-are-Automatic-Anchor-Tags?language=en_US) article.

1. Open Word, launch the Docusign Template Assistant add-in, and log in to your Docusign eSignature account.
2. Open the document, place the cursor where you want to insert a field or table, or highlight text to replace.
3. In the Docusign Template Assistant, go to the Fields tab, and select the Fields or Tables option.
4. Select the item that you want to insert.
5. Use Word formatting tools to apply desired formatting, such as bold, font size, or color.

   When the document is generated in Docusign eSignature, it retains your applied formatting.
6. (Optional) Review and edit column headers in the dynamic table as needed.

The sender field or dynamic table is inserted and formatted.

title: Insert Sender Fields and Dynamic Tables Into a Document
heading_path: Insert Sender Fields and Dynamic Tables Into a Document
text: Verify that sender fields and dynamic tables display correctly. For more information about previewing a document, see the [Preview a Generated Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=gke1680044621975.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Docusign Template Assistant for Word
text:
Read about how the Docusign Template Assistant for Word simplifies the creation of DOCX files and templates.

The Docusign Template Assistant for Word is an add-in for creating DOCX files and templates.

Note: The Docusign Template Assistant for
Word Add-in is available in the [Microsoft Office Store](https://appsource.microsoft.com/en-us/product/office/wa200005367?tab=overview).

title: Docusign Template Assistant for Word
text:
While you can prepare your DOCX files manually, this recommended add-in provides
significant benefits:

- Automatically writes and enhances syntax, including adding validation to sender fields.
- Supports field configuration so you can specify text, date, number
  fields, and dropdowns.
- Preview the generated document in Word to identify issues before uploading to Docusign.
- Upload your document to a new or existing template.

title: Docusign Template Assistant for Word
text: Watch this video to learn how to create a document generation template using the Docusign Template Assistant:

title: Error: Missing Field Brackets
text:
Learn how to resolve a document generation syntax error that occurs when your syntax is
missing data field brackets.

Document generation data fields must be in double curly brackets. If a field is missing one or
more brackets, it will not be included on the pre-fill page where the sender enters
values for the data fields.

title: Error: Missing Field Brackets
heading_path: Enclose all data fields in double curly brackets
text:
Template creator

1. Edit your .docx file to correct the syntax errors.
2. Check each inserted data field for proper placement in double curly
   brackets.

   Correct syntax: `{{ field_name }}`
   `{{Field_Name}}`

   Incorrect syntax: `{ field_name }`
   `{{ field_name }`
3. Upload the corrected .docx file to your eSignature template.

title: Error Code: JsonReaderException
text:
Troubleshoot the JsonReaderException error code.

A variety of formatting errors in your document generation syntax can lead to an error in
parsing JSON syntax.

Example: missing a comma in the SuppressParagraph tag
`{t:"SuppressParagraph" e: "FieldName = 'value'}`

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | {"tagIdentifier":"{t:\"SuppressParagraph\" e:\"FieldName = 'value'\"}", "errorCode":"JsonReaderException","message":"Unable to parse Json object. -- There was an error parsing this merge tag as json (check spacing, quotes, json syntax): After parsing a value an unexpected character was encountered: e. Path 't', line 1, position 23."} |
```

Template creator

This is a general issue with parsing
so the solution will vary from case to case.

title: Add a Rule to Show or Hide Conditional Content
heading_path: Add a Rule to Show or Hide Conditional Content
text: Learn how to add rules in the DocusignTemplate Assistant for Word to display conditional content based on sender field values.

title: Add a Rule to Show or Hide Conditional Content
heading_path: Add a Rule to Show or Hide Conditional Content
text:
- Your Docusign Template Assistant should already have the sender fields configured for your rules.
- Open the Word document and start the Docusign Template Assistant.
- Log in to any accessible Production or Demo account.

The Docusign Template Assistant for Word lets you add conditional content based on sender field values.

Conditional content may consist of regular text and other sender fields. Various logical operators, such as exact text matches or value comparisons, can be applied.

For example, if your offer letter includes a clause specific to a job title, you can add a rule to display the clause when the `Job Role` field is "Software Engineer".

1. Select the Rules tab, and select the Show content or Hide content option.
2. Complete the rule configuration.

   Field Name
   :   Select the sender field for the rule argument from the dropdown list of available fields in the Docusign Template Assistant.

       Note: The selected sender field is added to the pre-fill form for completion, even if it's not in your document.

   Operator
   :   Select the comparison operator for the rule. Text and Date fields support text matching (Matches, Doesn't match), while Number fields support text matching and numerical comparisons.

   Value
   :   Input the comparison value.
3. In your Word document, choose the content for the rule, and select Apply.

   ![Docusign Template Assistant for Word with a Show rule specified and applied to text in the document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/showcontentruleexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMDIsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.zQwDiRABkhr1i4b4_WhbflwSP1kI8ZuqD_eKTxH_VDI&_LANG=enus "Docusign Template Assistant for Word with a Show rule specified and applied to text in the document.")

The rule syntax is added to your document, displaying content only when the sender provides a valid value in the rule's data field.

title: Add a Rule to Show or Hide Conditional Content
heading_path: Add a Rule to Show or Hide Conditional Content
text: [Preview a Generated Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=gke1680044621975.html&_LANG=enus) to verify your rule's results. Rules are assessed using test values for sender fields, allowing you to test various scenarios.

title: Bulk Send With Document Generation
text:
Learn how to use bulk send with a document generation template.

Document generation supports bulk send. You can use bulk send to send many envelopes at once, each with customized data.

Starting with a document generation template, you import a bulk list with the individual envelope and recipient data as usual. The bulk list also contains the values for the document generation sender fields and dynamic tables. So for document generation with bulk send, the sender does not use the Add field data form to enter custom data before sending.

When preparing for a bulk send, consider the following requirements:

- Every sender field with the same field name must have the same value. This includes fields on different documents in the envelope.
- The maximum number of sender fields that can be used for a bulk send envelope is 100.

For more information about bulk send see [this help guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=wtn1643071711000&topicId=kjz1578456386508.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Prepare your bulk send list CSV file to provide the data for your sender fields and dynamic tables.
   1. Sender field requirements:

      - Sender field column header format: `Document Generation::Field_Name`
      - Each required sender field must have exactly one column in the CSV
      - For each row in the CSV, there must be a value for all required sender fields
   2. Dynamic table requirements:

      - Dynamic table column header format: `Dynamic Table::{Table Name}|{Column Name}`
      - Each column in the dynamic table must have a properly formatted column in the CSV
      - Each row in the CSV includes the dynamic table data for that bulk send envelope
      - Use the pipe symbol (|) as a delimiter to create rows in the dynamic table
      - Any spacing around the delimiter will be included in the dynamic table data
      - Example of dynamic table data:![Screenshot of a portion of a bulk send csv showing an example of dynamic table data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/bulksendcsvdynamictable.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.C9-wMkmCjP_8wUFvPjQIjyxIz3pTT2130wDi5LWTy_s&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a portion of a bulk send csv showing an example of dynamic table data.")
      - For the example data, the bulk send envelope generated from the first row of CSV data would yield a dynamic table with two rows:![Screenshot of a dynamic table generated from a bulk send list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/bulksenddynamictableexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.C9-wMkmCjP_8wUFvPjQIjyxIz3pTT2130wDi5LWTy_s&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a dynamic table generated from a bulk send list.")
2. Use your document generation template to start a new envelope.
3. In the Add recipients section, select Import Bulk List and import your prepared bulk send CSV file.
4. Review the data in the Bulk List Preview. You can complete or edit the imported column values as needed. Then select FINISH IMPORT.

   Your bulk recipient is added to the envelope. Values for the document generation sender fields and dynamic tables will be populated from the entries in the bulk send list; you will not see the usual Add field data form. These values are inserted seamlessly into the document, ensuring a well-formatted agreement and a professional look.
5. Finish preparing your envelope and send it.

   Note: Before sending the envelope, when you view your uploaded .docx file, you will see the document generation syntax. This preview does not show the final agreement with your custom data inserted. The data will be inserted when you send the envelope.

title: Bulk Send With Document Generation
text:
r uploaded .docx file, you will see the document generation syntax. This preview does not show the final agreement with your custom data inserted. The data will be inserted when you send the envelope.

Your bulk send is sent. The document generation sender fields and dynamic tables are processed using the bulk send CSV file data.

title: Create a Dynamic Table in the Template Assistant
heading_path: Create a Dynamic Table in the Template Assistant
text: Learn how to create dynamic tables using the DocusignTemplate Assistant for Word, which automatically adjusts to the data provided.

title: Create a Dynamic Table in the Template Assistant
heading_path: Create a Dynamic Table in the Template Assistant
text:
Dynamic tables have these limitations:

- Formulas and inline calculations aren't supported. Only API or CSV values are accepted.
- Special characters aren't accepted in table field names.

Use the Docusign Template Assistant for Word to create dynamic tables that automatically adjust to the data provided. For more information about dynamic tables, see the [Dynamic Tables for Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=zhi1724987563409.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

1. Open a Word document, launch the Template Assistant add-in, and log in to your eSignature account.
2. In the Docusign Template Assistant, go to the Fields tab.
3. Select the + button, and select Create table.

   In the Insert dynamic table window, complete the details:

   Table Name
   :   Enter a table name for identification. This name displays only in the Template Assistant and is not visible in the final document.

   FIELD NAME
   :   Each field is a table column, with the entered name as the default column heading, editable upon table insertion.

       Note: Field names cannot contain any of the following special characters

       : # % ! ? @ ^ & \* ( ) - + = [ ] { } : ; / ,
4. Finalize your table by adding and arranging field items to form columns.

   ![Screenshot of the Insert dynamic table window in the Template Assistant, showing table name and field name columns being configured before adding the table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/insert-table-WTA.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwOTUsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.a6qzVMa3BgL-LBYXH_Ram1iiU3LQpJY0SnG5zqwR1jk&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Insert dynamic table window in the Template Assistant, showing table name and field name columns being configured before adding the table.")
5. Select Add Table to add your new dynamic table to the Template Assistant.

   ![Screenshot of an example dynamic table defined in the Template Assistant and inserted into a document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/dynamictableexamples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwOTUsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.a6qzVMa3BgL-LBYXH_Ram1iiU3LQpJY0SnG5zqwR1jk&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of an example dynamic table defined in the Template Assistant and inserted into a document.")

Your dynamic table is added to the list of tables for the selected agreement type in the Template Assistant. You can [insert the table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=kgg1680241042480.html&_LANG=enus) into any Word document.

title: Error: Data Field Is Not Included on Data-Entry Form
heading_path: Error: Data Field Is Not Included on Data-Entry Form
text:
Troubleshoot a merge error for an unrecognized field.

If a field is incorrectly formatted such that it is not recognized as a field, the syntax will
remain in the document as is. Since it is not a valid data field, it won't be
included on the data-entry form for the sender. No error will be returned because it
is not recognized as a field. It is up to the sender to notice the field is not
included in the data-entry form, and to spot the faulty syntax when reviewing the
final generated document.

Example: A data field is missing one curly bracket `{residential_address}}`.
The field is incorrectly formatted and unrecognizable, so it is ignored in the
data merge.

Sender and Template owner

1. Sender: Review the final generated document before sending and locate
   the invalid syntax.
2. Template owner: Open the source .docx file for the document generation
   template you are using. Correct the invalid syntax to conform to
   document generation syntax rules.
3. Template owner: Edit the template and replace the .docx document with
   the corrected file.

title: Conditional Rules and Syntax for Suppressing Text
heading_path: Conditional Rules and Syntax for Suppressing Text
text:
In addition to conditional statements to show content, you can specify to remove text
from a document based on conditional arguments.

You can specify conditional rules to hide blocks of text based on the value of a data
field.

title: Conditional Rules and Syntax for Suppressing Text
heading_path: Conditional Rules and Syntax for Suppressing Text
text:
The format for suppressing text requires verbose syntax.

`{{ t:"SuppressParagraph", e:"fieldName = ‘value’"}}Text that will be removed
if the field name inputted by the sender is ‘value’`

- `t:` Indicates that it is a SuppressParagraph tag.
- `e:` The condition that must be met for the suppress paragraph to
  be invoked.
- The text after the `t:` and the `e:` must be in
  double quotes.
- There must be a comma separating the `t:` and `e:`
  parameters.

title: Conditional Rules and Syntax for Suppressing Text
heading_path: Conditional Rules and Syntax for Suppressing Text
text:
Line breaks
:   - Using SuppressParagraph will remove any text that is in the same
      paragraph as the suppress syntax. That is, when there is no explicit
      line break between the expression and the text to be removed.

      In
      the following example, there is a line break before the second
      sentence, so it will not be removed.
:   ```
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    |  | {{ t:"SuppressParagraph", e:"fieldName = ‘value’"}}Text that will be removed if the field name inputted by the sender is ‘value’. |
    |  | This sentence will not be removed because it comes after a line break |
    ```
:   - If there is a line break between the SupressParagraph tag and the
      conditional text, you will get an error.

      In the following
      example, the SuppressParagraph tag is not in a paragraph of
      text; there is a line break between it and the text that follows
      it. This will generate a syntax error.

    ```
    |  |  |
    | --- | --- |
    |  | {{ t:"SuppressParagraph", e:"fieldName = ‘value’"}} |
    |  | Text that will not be removed because it’s not in the same paragraph as the SuppressParagraph tag |
    ```

title: Error: Incorrect Placement of SuppressParagraph
text:
Troubleshoot a document generation syntax error that occurs when a SuppressParagraph tag
is in the wrong place.

A SuppressParagraph tag in the wrong place will cause an error when you upload a document
generation .docx file.

The SuppressParagraph tag is not positioned as part of a paragraph. Example:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | {"tagIdentifier": "{t:\"SuppressParagraph\", e: \"fieldName = 'value'\"}", |
|  | "errorCode": "InvalidPlacement", |
|  | "message": "Invalid placement of a Merge Tag in the template. – SuppressParagraph: This tag must be placed in a paragraph."} |
```

Template creator

Ensure that the SuppressParagraph
tag is part of a paragraph. The tag must have text next to it with no
intervening line breaks.

title: Permission Profiles
text:
Read about how administrators use permission profiles to manage levels of access for a
number of users. Permission profiles are sets of permissions that administrators assign to
individual users. By managing the permission profiles, administrators can make changes to
multiple users simultaneously.

A permission profile is a group of settings that determines the behavior and actions
available for a user. Administrators assign a permission profile to a user when the user is
added to an account.

Permission profiles make it easier to manage permission settings for a large number of users.
Administrators can modify the profile settings, which are applied to all users with that
profile, instead of changing permission settings for each user individually.

Docusign eSignature account administration includes three default
permission profiles:

- DS Admin: can access all eSignature features and can manage all account settings and users
  for an account.
- DS Sender: can access to basic eSignature features. No administrator access. DS Senders
  can only send envelopes and use templates and PowerForms. They cannot use bulk send, create
  templates, or manage envelopes through the API.
- DS Viewer: can view and sign envelopes in eSignature. No access to sending envelopes.

Although you cannot modify or delete the default permission profiles, you can copy them to use as
templates for other permission profiles.

Note: If your account includes the Advanced
Administration module, you can use the delegated administration options to create permission
profiles that have limited administrator capabilities.

title: Troubleshooting Merged Data Errors
text:
Get help with common problems encountered with merged data provided by the sender of a
document generation envelope.

If there are errors when trying to merge the data provided by the sender, they will appear on
the document as red text with a yellow highlight. An error message banner identifies the
number of merge errors and provides a link to download a .JSON errors file.

![Example of merge error.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/docgenmergeerror.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxNTgsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.efKucNOrFevxXTvtooIaaYys1-9hLf_3BnBsO9tnP2I&_LANG=enus "Example of merge error.")

title: Create a Template from a Document
heading_path: Create a Template from a Document
text: Learn how to prepare a DOCX file and upload it to Docusign using the Template Assistant to create a document generation template.

title: Create a Template from a Document
heading_path: Create a Template from a Document
text:
- Open the Microsoft Word document and launch the Docusign Template Assistant in your Docusign account.
- Log in to your Docusign account with permissions to create templates.
- Recommended: Use the [Preview a Generated Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=gke1680044621975.html&_LANG=enus) feature to check and correct errors in your Word document before uploading it to an eSignature template.

Prepare your DOCX file with content, sender fields, and optional rules. Create a new template or add it to an existing one. Senders will use the template to pre-fill dynamic data and generate the final document for signing.

Important: Use the Docusign Template Assistant to upload documents with syntax. Direct uploads to a Docusign template won't recognize the syntax.

1. In the Docusign Template Assistant, select Upload document to open a new window.

   ![Upload document button in the Template Assistant window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/upload-document-button-WTA.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMDcsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.LfWxcqYANrlkNcY1cgxdK2gUVkqaKsIiaTLMqr6ZBhY&_LANG=enus "Upload document button in the Template Assistant window.")
2. Choose to upload the file as a document or envelope template.
   - For a document template, create a new document template or replace an existing one.
   - For an envelope template, select Next, and create a new envelope template or add to an existing one.

   ![Screenshot of the Upload window from Template Assistant.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/uploaddocgen.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMDcsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.LfWxcqYANrlkNcY1cgxdK2gUVkqaKsIiaTLMqr6ZBhY&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Upload window from Template Assistant.")

    Your envelope template opens to the prepare step with the document labeled "Document Generation" in the documents section. Your document template displays in the Document Template library, also labeled "Document Generation".

   ![The Template prepare file with an uploaded DOCX file with document generation data fields, bearing the "Document Generation" indicator.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/genfile.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMDcsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.LfWxcqYANrlkNcY1cgxdK2gUVkqaKsIiaTLMqr6ZBhY&_LANG=enus "The Template prepare file with an uploaded DOCX file with document generation data fields, bearing the \"Document Generation\" indicator.")
3. Add recipient roles and message details to complete the template preparation.

   The document displays sender fields and conditional statements from the uploaded file.
4. Add additional eSignature fields as needed. Use AutoPlace to automatically position fields next to a specified character string, ensuring they remain correctly placed even as content adjusts.

Your document is now a dynamic template with all fields, dynamic tables, and logic rules applied, ready to generate and send personalized agreements. Before sending for signing, add any remaining fields and settings to finalize the template.

title: Create a Template from a Document
heading_path: Create a Template from a Document
text: Use your template to [Send a Document Generation Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=ekg1679981354189.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Create Sender Fields With JSON Syntax in the Template Assistant
heading_path: Create Sender Fields With JSON Syntax in the Template Assistant
text:
Learn how to quickly add and share sender fields to the Docusign Template Assistant Add-in for Word by pasting in JSON data.

Using JSON syntax in the Docusign Template Assistant offers two key benefits for managing sender fields:

- **Quick Creation**: Paste existing JSON field data into the Code tab to quickly populate the Docusign Template Assistant, streamlining document setup.
- **Easy Sharing**: Configure sender fields, copy the JSON from the Code tab, and share it externally. Others can paste this JSON into their Docusign Template Assistant to replicate the fields.

1. Open Word, launch the Docusign Template Assistant Add-in, and log in to your Docusign account.
2. Go to the Code tab.

   ![Code tab in the Template Assistant Add-in showing the JSON editor area for sender fields](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/code-tab-WTA.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.BAii3NxOShzyImQ8IhvvMr5sutSYyhiQKOeB_W3Vbmo&_LANG=enus "Code tab in the Template Assistant Add-in showing the JSON editor area for sender fields")
3. Copy and paste your JSON data into the Insert JSON box. Avoid overwriting existing fields unless you intend to replace them.
4. Select Insert.

The JSON-defined sender fields are added to the Fields tab in the Docusign Template Assistant. You can [select and insert them into your document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=kgg1680241042480.html&_LANG=enus). Overwritten fields are removed from the sender fields list.

title: Upload Field Data for a Dynamic Table
heading_path: Upload Field Data for a Dynamic Table
text:
Learn how to send a document generation envelope that includes a dynamic table. As the
sender, you provide field data for the table by uploading a CSV file.

When you use a document generation template that includes a document with a dynamic table, you
provide the table data by uploading a CSV file. The CSV file must include all column
headings specified for the table.

The system validates that all the column headings in your uploaded file are present
and correct. However, there is no validation on the table data itself.

You can download an example CSV file that includes the required column headings; or you can
create your own file from scratch.

Note: With the Document Generation add-on, developers can use the
API to transfer data from external systems of record. See [this API documentation](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/documents/document-generation/#dynamic-tables) for more information.

1. Use a document generation template to start an envelope as described in [Send a Document Generation Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=ekg1679981354189.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how a sender uses a template for document generation.").
2. When the Add field data form displays, complete the sender
   fields as required.

   ![Screenshot of the Add field data form with a dynamic table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/addfielddatedynamictable.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMTgsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.tC_DYkNQ6rdT1AqruMDSaikmcrThrLU5vXeEDZ6jmbM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Add field data form with a dynamic table.")
3. Upload a CSV file containing the data for each dynamic table.

   - Each table requires its own CSV file with the table data.
   - The CSV file must contain the column headings as specified for your
     dynamic table.
   - You can download the sample CSV, which includes the necessary
     formatting.

   Example CSV data:

   ![Screenshot of example csv data for a dynamic table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/examplecsvdynamictable.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMTgsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.tC_DYkNQ6rdT1AqruMDSaikmcrThrLU5vXeEDZ6jmbM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of example csv data for a dynamic table.")

   A green check mark indicates that your file was uploaded successfully. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/example_uploaded_csv.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgxMTgsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.tC_DYkNQ6rdT1AqruMDSaikmcrThrLU5vXeEDZ6jmbM&_LANG=enus)

The dynamic table in your generated document is populated with
the data from the uploaded CSV.

title: Error: Invalid Field Name
text:
Learn how to resolve a document generation syntax error that occurs when a data field
name is not formatted correctly.

An invalid field name will cause an error when you upload a document generation .docx
file.

Sample error:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  |  |
|  | {"tagIdentifier": "{{First Name}}", |
|  | "errorCode": "Undefined", |
|  | "message": "There was an error parsing this merge tag as json (check spacing, quotes, json syntax): Invalid character after parsing property name. Expected ':' but got: N. Path '', line 1, position 7."} |
|  |  |
```

Document generation data field names must not have any spaces.

title: Error: Invalid Field Name
heading_path: Ensure all data field names are formatted correctly
text:
Template creator

1. Edit your .docx file to correct the syntax errors.
2. Locate the inserted data field name that is identified in the error
   message for improper formatting and correct as needed.

   Correct syntax: `{{First_Name}}`

   Incorrect syntax: `{{First Name}}`
3. Upload the corrected .docx file to your eSignature template.

title: Add a Third-Party Field or Table to a Template
heading_path: Add a Third-Party Field or Table to a Template
text:
- Install the extension app for your third-party system and authorize a connection to your account.
- Create a template with a .docx file and edit the template using the ATB.

With the agreement template builder (ATB), you can insert third-party data fields and tables as sender fields into your templates. These fields act as dynamic placeholders that are automatically populated with data from a specific record in your third-party system when an agreement is generated.

To add third-party data fields, you first select the source object. While you can select only one **source object** per document, you can select multiple additional **related objects**. This gives you access to fields from multiple source objects, stemming from the selected source object for the document.

Note: While Docusign integrates with many systems of record, for illustration purposes, this topic presents how to add Salesforce object fields.

1. In your template editor, select the Sender role, and then select the Agreement Fields dropdown and chose your third-party connection. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/select_connection.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNzAsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.QSdCFXZmo6L5gX0LoOYwCAYNSdBa0x8EGgbpNSrKxHc&_LANG=enus)

   This selection reflects the third-party system you installed and authorized. This help topic is using Salesforce as an example.
2. Search for and select the source object to use for the agreement.

   ![Selecting the source object.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/select_source_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNzAsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.QSdCFXZmo6L5gX0LoOYwCAYNSdBa0x8EGgbpNSrKxHc&_LANG=enus "Selecting the source object.")

   The list of fields for the selected object are presented.
3. Position your cursor where you want to place a field, then select the desired field.
4. (Optional) Select the placed field to open the properties window.
   1. Add a field description.
   2. Enable the Required Field toggle to make the field required.
   3. Select Save.
5. To add a table:
   1. Select the Tables tab.

      The Tables tab displays all related objects that share a many-to-one relationship with your selected source object.
   2. Select the table that you want to add.

      The Create new Dynamic Table window opens.
   3. Select the + symbol for the fields that you want
      to add as columns for your table. Drag to reorder columns as needed.
   4. Select Create.
   5. To edit the table after you create it, select the table and then select
      the Configure table gear icon.
6. To add a field or table for a related object:
   1. Scroll to the bottom of the list of Salesforce fields and select Related Objects.
   2. Position your cursor where you want to insert the field or table and select it from the list.
   3. Adjust the properties as needed.
   4. Select Save.

      ![Example of a chain of multiple related objects to the document source object, "Account".](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/related_objects.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNzAsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.QSdCFXZmo6L5gX0LoOYwCAYNSdBa0x8EGgbpNSrKxHc&_LANG=enus "Example of a chain of multiple related objects to the document source object, \"Account\".")

Your third-party field is added to your template document.

title: Preview a Generated Document
text:
To see how your generated document will look, you can use the preview function. For the preview, you manually enter data for your sender fields. This data includes all agreement fields, custom fields, and third-party fields present on your document. You must provide a value for required fields.

Note: For third-party fields, actual field values are not available in the ATB preview. You can see how your document will look with sample data that you enter manually. But actual values from your connected account are available only when you generate an agreement from the template as part of a workflow created with Workflow Builder.

1. In your draft template, select Preview at the top right of the ATB.
2. Enter sample data to populate the sender fields. The experience is slightly
   different for document templates and envelope templates.

   - **Envelope Templates** present a form to first add the data and
     then display the generated document. Enter your data and then select
     Review.
   - **Document Templates** provide a side-by-side view with and
     Add field data on the left and your
     document on the right. Your document updates as you enter field
     data.

     ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/dt_preview.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNzQsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.JwpRMgLYV8D_eI1BQjEhqKi5DvB_pVQoikoiQnxwtE4&_LANG=enus)

title: Add a Custom Agreement Field
text:
Agreement fields are the fields associated with the agreement type. Each agreement type has
a defined set of standard fields. You can add new **custom fields** to an agreement
type. You can also select any custom field created on the account by you or other
account users. Adding or selecting other custom fields creates an association with the
current agreement type. This association means the fields will be included by default
the next time anyone on your account uses that type.

Note:

The [Agreement Type and Field Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=uie1724446326038.html&_LANG=enus) guide provides an
overview of how Docusign Agreement Manager categorizes documents using **standard and
custom agreement types**. It explains how to use the **Types and
Fields** management tool to standardize data collection, use AI for
auto-suggesting agreement types, and tailor metadata extraction to meet specific
organizational needs.

1. In the ATB for your template, select the Sender role and review the agreement type selected for your document to confirm it's a valid selection. Change the type if necessary.
2. Position your cursor where you want to insert the custom field, and select + Insert Items and choose Field.
3. In the Sender Field details, first search by field name to see if a suitable custom field already exists on your account.
   1. Use the dropdown to scroll through the saved custom fields or enter a search string.
   2. If a suitable field exists, select it.
   3. (Optional) Edit the field description and adjust the Required field
      selection as needed. (These are the only settings that you can adjust
      for an existing custom field.)
   4. Select Save.

      The field is inserted into your document and added to the list of fields for the
      agreement type.
4. If the field doesn't exist, select the Create option and complete the Sender Field details:
   1. **Field Description**. (Optional) Explain the primary function of
      the custom field. Describe what kind of data that it will hold and how
      it should be used.
   2. **Required Field**. Mark the checkbox to make the field required. If this option is unchecked, the field is optional.
   3. **Field Type**. Select the appropriate option: Text, Date, Dropdown, or Number.
5. Select Save.

Your custom field is inserted into your document and added to the list of fields for the current agreement type.

title: Error: Invalid Conditional Statement
text:
Troubleshoot a document generation syntax error that occurs when a conditional statement
is not formatted correctly.

A conditional statement with improper formatting will cause an error when you upload
a document generation .docx file.

`{{if statement}}` does not have a valid
`{{endif}}`. Example:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | "errorCode": "Undefined", |
|  | "message": "There was an error parsing this merge tag as json (check spacing, quotes, json syntax): Invalid character after parsing property name. Expected ':' but got: N. Path '', line 1, position 7."} |
```

Template creator

Ensure that there are no formatting
issues with the `{{endif}}`, such as a missing
bracket.

`{{if statement}}` is not valid. Common examples:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | {“tagIdentifier": "{\"type\":\"EndConditional\"}", |
|  | "errorCode": "EndTagWithoutStartTag", |
|  | "message": "End tag without matching start tag. -- Error: EndConditional without matching Conditional"} |
```

Template creator

Ensure that there are no formatting
issues with the `{{if statement}}` .

title: Error: Value Provided Does Not Pass Validation
heading_path: Error: Value Provided Does Not Pass Validation
text:
Troubleshoot a merge error for a validation issue.

Currently, there is no upfront validation for document generation; senders can enter any value
for a data field. If a value does not meet the expected validation for a field, it
will result in a merge error.

Note: In the future, we plan to build error handling into the
form-filling experience so the sender is prevented from proceeding if there are
issues with the data provided.

Example: A document has a conditional statement that expects a number `{{if
$number(count) = 30}}`. The sender enters a non-numeric value for
the data field "count", resulting in a merge error.

Sender

Review the document preview and
locate the highlighted merge error. These errors display in red text with a
yellow highlight. If needed, download the .JSON error file for more information
about the merge errors. Navigate back to the data-entry form and correct the
values that triggered the error.

title: Edit a Sender Field in the Template Assistant
heading_path: Edit a Sender Field in the Template Assistant
text:
Edit sender fields in the Docusign Template Assistant by updating configuration details or modifying JSON syntax.

Edit an existing sender field by updating the field configuration details or modifying the JSON syntax, which is automatically generated when you [add a sender field](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=dtw1680044374844.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Open the Docusign Template Assistant in Word and log in to your Docusign account.
2. Go to the Fields tab, and select the pencil icon next to the sender field to edit.
3. Update the configuration and select Save.
4. (Optional) To edit a field's JSON syntax, go to the Code tab, update the syntax, and select Insert.

Your sender field and JSON syntax are updated.

title: Dynamic Tables for Document Generation
text:
Read about dynamic tables. Dynamic tables are designed for agreements that include
tables with a variable number of rows, depending on the data provided.

title: Dynamic Tables for Document Generation
text:
Many agreements require tables to present data. Creating and formatting tables is
time-consuming and prone to manual errors. This is especially true when the number
of rows and information in a table changes to customize each agreement. With agreement preparation, you can create dynamic tables that adjust to fit
the data provided, adding as many rows as needed.

Data for the table is specified when a document is generated. For Docusign IAM customers, table data can come from a third-party system record (such as Salesforce) or from an uploaded CSV file. Docusign eSignature customers can upload a CSV file only.

Note: ATB also provides an option to insert **static tables**. Static tables are defined in the template with a fixed number of rows and columns. Static tables are useful when the number of rows and columns is predetermined and remains constant across different documents. This makes static tables suitable for standardized reports, fixed-format documents, or any scenario where the data structure is stable and predictable.

title: Dynamic Tables for Document Generation
text:
Here are some common use case examples for dynamic tables:

Purchase Orders and Invoices
:   The number of rows in the table varies depending on the number of items in
    the order.

Grant Applications
:   The number of rows in the table varies depending on the number of
    applications.

Project Status Report
:   The number of rows depends on the size of the project and what the
    milestones are.

title: Dynamic Tables for Document Generation
heading_path: How do you create dynamic tables?
text:
You can create dynamic tables in your templates with either the Agreement Template Builder or
the Docusign Template Assistant.

See the following help topics for detailed instructions:

- [Insert a Dynamic Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=kbb1723763558691.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add a dynamic table with the agreement template builder. Dynamic tables can be populated with as many rows as necessary when the agreement is generated.")
- [Create a Dynamic Table in the Template Assistant](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=nhd1712957935412.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create dynamic tables using the DocusignTemplate Assistant for Word, which automatically adjusts to the data provided.")

Note: For Docusign eSignature customers, sending documents with dynamic tables requires purchasing the
Document Generation for eSignature add-on. Users without the add-on can send up to
five trial envelopes that include Document Generation add-on features. This feature is included with Docusign IAM plans.

title: Dynamic Tables for Document Generation
text:
Dynamic tables offer improved data accuracy over building tables manually. You can
use API endpoints to transfer data from systems of record directly into agreements
to generate tables. Or if you are using the eSignature web application, you upload a
CSV file with the data to populate the table. Transmitting table data to your
agreement in either way reduces the potential for human error.

Note: For developer information on how to use the
API to transfer data from external systems of record, see [this API documentation](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/documents/document-generation/#dynamic-tables).

title: Dynamic Tables for Document Generation
text:
There are a few limitations to consider when building dynamic tables.

- You can define the data for dynamic tables with either Agreement Fields (standard and custom) or third-party fields (such as Salesforce fields). Data for the table can come from only one source.
- In ATB, tables can contain up to 10 columns from either Agreement Fields or third-party fields.
- In WTA, tables can contain up to 20 columns from either Agreement Fields or third-party fields.

title: Add Conditional Rules To Show or Hide Content
heading_path: Add Conditional Rules To Show or Hide Content
text:
Learn how to show or hide content dynamically based on values entered in sender fields.

With the agreement template builder (ATB), you can use conditional rules to make a single template
work for a multitude of situations. Rather than building custom templates to cover
different agreement variables, you can add conditional rules. These rules can show
or hide content based on values entered in sender fields.

Important: Conditional rules in ATB apply to **complete paragraphs only**. You cannot apply a rule to a fragment of a clause or a specific string of text within a larger paragraph.

**Example: Vacation Policy**

In an offer letter, you might have different vacation policies that depend on where an employee lives. To handle this, you first add a **sender field** named "State" to your template. When a sender prepares the agreement, they enter the employee's state of residence into this field.

Because conditional rules rely on these values but apply to **complete paragraphs only**, you must create two distinct versions of the policy:

- **Paragraph A:** Specifies 15 days for California employees (Rule: If State = California, Show).
- **Paragraph B:** Specifies 12 days for all other states (Rule: If State ≠ California, Show).

The Rule Builder uses the "State" sender field to determine which entire block of text to include.

![Screenshot of the Rule Builder configured to show information based on a sender field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/rulebuilderconfig.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNTQsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.wVzuJWC_-x8HWb8ahLidCJa6z7YJSbNdwnqcIRzN9Mo&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Rule Builder configured to show information based on a sender field.")

Note: Conditional rules are part of the advanced features in ATB. Docusign IAM customers have full access to advanced features. For eSignature customers, these features are available
with the Document Generation add-on. All eSiganture accounts have access to basic document
generation and the option to try these advanced features. The add-on is required to
send more than five trial envelopes from a document generation template that
includes advanced features.

1. Create a document generation template using ATB.
2. Highlight the complete block of text in your document to make conditional and select the
   logic rule icon to the right.

   You can also access the rule builder feature through the tool bar icon or by
   hovering over the right margin of your document. ![Screenshot of ways to access the rule builder.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/als1679428547895/page/images/atbrulebuilder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwNTQsInNoZWFmIjoiYWxzMTY3OTQyODU0Nzg5NSJ9.wVzuJWC_-x8HWb8ahLidCJa6z7YJSbNdwnqcIRzN9Mo&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of ways to access the rule builder.")
3. In the Rule Builder select whether to Apply all or Apply any conditions to satisfy the rule.
4. For the first IF statement, drag the sender field from
   your list of fields that you want to use. Then select the operator and enter a
   value.
5. To add more conditions, select And if.
6. Select whether to Show or Hide
   content and verify the text to apply.
7. Complete the Otherwise... portion of the rule. This
   determines what is shown or hidden if the rule conditions are not met.
8. Select Save.
